notes: Turn: openings:plaintiff. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only evidence is case-packet ev_54607018b65c_market-rules (sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6), 1650-byte markdown. Contents: market asks whether Israel invaded Syria in 2024; resolves Yes if Israel commenced a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Syria between 2024-09-12 and 2024-12-31 11:59 PM ET; Golan Heights count as Israeli territory; official confirmation by Syria/Israel/UN/UNSC permanent member or consensus credible reporting can resolve. Packet says Polymarket resolved Yes because Dec. 17 Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon and announced Israeli forces would remain until a security arrangement; credible reporting had placed Israeli forces on the peak Dec. 8; rationale treats this as intent to establish control. It also says Syrian peak of Mount Hermon is not Golan Heights. Outside investigation for source targeting only, not admitted evidence: found likely primary targets Israeli PMO Dec. 17 page gov.il/en/pages/event-hermon171224 and Dec. 8 page gov.il/en/pages/event-golan081224; UNDOF mandate page for 1974 disengagement framework; corroboration leads PBS/AP, Times of Israel, Reuters/Al Arabiya, VOA. Adverse theory: Israel will frame deployment as temporary and defensive after Assad collapse; defense may dispute 'offensive' and control intent, and may exploit Golan/Hermon geography. Plaintiff theory: market does not require annexation or permanence; taking over positions in Syrian territory and maintaining troops until a security arrangement is enough control intent. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with searches, source targets, adverse checks, and next-phase evidence plan. Opening will rely only on record facts and frame outside targets as future proof, not as admitted support.

Defendant opening work notes. Opportunity: openings:defendant. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Only visible evidence is ev_54607018b65c_market-rules, case-packet markdown, sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6, 1650 bytes. Full packet read; it contains market question, rules, Golan carveout, authority standard, and Polymarket's disputed Yes rationale. It does not attach or authenticate the underlying official Israeli statement, UN materials, reporting, maps, geolocation, or source chain. Native investigation/search performed before filing: searched UN/UNDOF, Israeli PMO, and credible reporting leads around Dec. 8/17 2024 Mount Hermon/buffer-zone events. Leads found: Israeli PMO Dec. 17 Hermon page with 'until another arrangement' language; Israeli PMO Dec. 8 Golan statement framing IDF action as temporary defensive position; UNDOF press statement saying IDF movements/presence in area of separation and violation of 1974 Disengagement Agreement; UN News/DPPA withdrawal concern; secondary reporting from Times of Israel/AP/Politico/VOA. Because openings do not permit evidence submission, these are not cited as admitted proof in the opening; they inform theory and later evidence plan only. Defense theory: plaintiff must prove not just presence/incursion/violation/airstrikes, but a military offensive intended to establish control over a non-Golan portion of Syria in the date window. Current packet proves rules and a rationale, not underlying facts. Even if later sources show IDF presence, intent and characterization remain central: temporary defensive/security-arrangement posture may not satisfy 'offensive intended to establish control.' Geography and source chain are decisive: Golan counts as Israeli territory; need distinguish Golan, area of separation, and Syrian peak. Later evidence targets preserved in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md: official PMO Dec. 17 and Dec. 8 pages, UNDOF press statement, UN reports, official/credible map/geography evidence, adverse official characterizations.

Arguments:plaintiff notes. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat; no new evidence before plaintiff submissions. Defendant opening focuses on geography, 'offensive,' and temporary/defensive control intent. Investigation: direct curl to gov.il Dec. 17 and Dec. 8 PMO pages was Cloudflare-blocked; direct curl to UNDOF mandate was CloudFront 403. Used web search/open and accessible mirror/source pages; preserved limitations in evidence descriptions. Submitted four source items: ev_87a2be06b06f_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-87a2be06b06f (PMO Dec.17 Mount Hermon statement via GlobalSecurity mirror, sha256 87a2be06b06fc76710dd9336bdc0cfce985794060222985d955feb28dfe34ec4); ev_96725428f566_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-96725428f566 (Times of Israel Dec.9 article excerpt, sha256 96725428f566b964f4a1aa288143db8e6502368786eb808a7e9602803209751e); ev_26cf1a5ab33e_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-26cf1a5ab33e (UNDOF mandate/disengagement framework excerpt, sha256 26cf1a5ab33eda16e89f5fe06727f7b5782509288b64c270c0cc818fdbceb3d9); ev_2aa6c6bb7f84_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-2aa6c6bb7f84 (AP Dec.17 report excerpt, sha256 2aa6c6bb7f84ce5cac11fb97a9beb4088bd9846ca54ce8b2ba032704020f986f). Final list_evidence confirms all visible. Merits theory: market rule requires only offensive intended to establish control over any Syrian portion; ToI/AP show capture/deployment inside Syria at Syrian Mount Hermon and buffer zone in Dec. 2024; PMO mirror provides official Israeli continued deployment/arrangement statement; UNDOF shows area is between Israel and Syria under 1974 disengagement, not ordinary Israeli territory. Adverse analysis: temporary/defensive language is real, but temporary military takeover/continued deployment still establishes control for market purposes; current access limitation reduces pristine official-source weight but is mitigated by official-text mirror plus AP/ToI. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant arguments work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. New plaintiff evidence read exactly: ev_87a2be06b06f PMO Dec.17 mirror; ev_96725428f566 Times of Israel Dec.9; ev_26cf1a5ab33e UNDOF mandate excerpt; ev_2aa6c6bb7f84 AP Dec.17. Assessment: plaintiff now has strong evidence of Israeli deployment/entry in UNDOF buffer zone / Syrian side of Mount Hermon, so location is not the best defense. Remaining decisive issue is whether the record proves the specific market standard: a military offensive intended to establish control, rather than a limited, temporary, defensive security deployment/ceasefire-zone breach after Syrian army abandonment. Plaintiff evidence itself preserves temporary/defensive framing. Provenance issues: PMO Dec.17 is GlobalSecurity mirror because gov.il blocked direct curl; UNDOF mandate is indexed/captured excerpt because direct page had CloudFront 403. Native investigation: tried curl gov.il Dec.8 PMO statement, got Cloudflare block. Searched Dec.8 temporary defensive position. Found and opened CRS report RL33487 on EveryCRSReport; relevant lines 389-396 summarize Israeli entry into UNDOF buffer zone and Israel's letter to UN stating limited and temporary measures, IDF deployed temporarily in a few points and limited capacity east of Line A; CRS also preserves adverse later material about long-term infrastructure and Jan.2025 Katz indefinite language. Submitted defense evidence accepted as ev_9ac4fbc4020a_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-9ac4fbc4020a, sha256 9ac4fbc4020a58d247ab9ec04d1432dd6053323afceefc005f8b6b81b5193867. Argument plan: concede ground deployment where record supports it; argue plaintiff conflates presence/takeover language with market-required offensive intent to establish control; emphasize temporary defensive posture, prevention of hostile embedding, abandoned positions, limited points east of Line A, and source/provenance limits. Offer defense CRS evidence and include concise search/access ledger technical report.

Rebuttal:plaintiff notes. Scanned list_evidence and read defendant evidence ev_9ac4fbc4020a_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-9ac4fbc4020a (CRS excerpt, sha256 9ac4fbc4020a58d247ab9ec04d1432dd6053323afceefc005f8b6b81b5193867). Defendant's strongest point is temporary/limited/defensive language. Exact CRS content also confirms key plaintiff points: as Assad regime collapsed on Dec. 8, 2024, Israeli military forces entered the UNDOF buffer zone established by the 1974 Israel-Syria Disengagement Agreement; Syrian authorities sought removal from areas within and beyond the zone; Israel said limited/temporary measures east of Line A; CRS states Netanyahu told press Israel's seizure of control over the UNDOF zone was a temporary defensive position until suitable arrangement found; later reports suggested long-term infrastructure and Jan. 2025 Katz said forces intend to remain at Mount Hermon/security zone indefinitely. Rebuttal plan: no new evidence needed; use defendant's exhibit as admission that control/seizure occurred during market window. Argument: market asks intended to establish control over any portion, not permanent conquest or non-defensive motive; temporary/defensive limits duration/rationale but does not negate takeover/control.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected record with get_case/list_evidence; no new plaintiff evidence submitted in rebuttal. Plaintiff re-offered defendant CRS excerpt as PX-R1 and argues the phrase 'seizure of control' is decisive. Read ev_9ac4fbc4020a again in this opportunity because exact wording matters. Evidence confirms CRS says Israeli military forces entered the UNDOF buffer zone, Israel described measures in a UN letter as limited and temporary and IDF deployed temporarily in a few points and limited capacity east of Line A, and Netanyahu told press Israel's seizure of control over the UNDOF zone was a temporary defensive position until a suitable arrangement was found. Surrebuttal plan: answer strongest rebuttal point; concede temporary local control if necessary but argue plaintiff still must prove military offensive intended to establish control. Plaintiff's reading makes every temporary security hold a qualifying invasion and reads 'offensive' and 'intended' out of the market. Emphasize all control/takeover language is embedded in limited/temporary/defensive/security-contingency context after Assad collapse and Syrian army abandonment. No new evidence needed.

Closing:plaintiff notes. Final record scan done with get_case/list_evidence/stat. No new evidence in closing. Record evidence: market packet ev_54607018b65c; plaintiff PMO mirror ev_87a2be06b06f; ToI Dec.9 ev_96725428f566; UNDOF framework ev_26cf1a5ab33e; AP Dec.17 ev_2aa6c6bb7f84; defendant CRS ev_9ac4fbc4020a. Defendant surrebuttal concedes entry and temporary local control but argues market phrase requires more. Closing plan: synthesize only existing record; emphasize any-portion standard and preponderance; argue temporary/limited/defensive limits motive/duration but not intent to take/hold control. Provenance: access limits exist but convergence across AP, ToI, PMO mirror, UNDOF, and CRS, plus defendant's own exhibit uses entered/seizure of control.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant. Reviewed prompt, get_case, list_evidence; no new evidence after surrebuttal. Plaintiff closing argues record proves Syria-side location, capture/takeover/seized positions, and continued deployment until security arrangement; says temporary/defensive/limited language only limits duration or motive and does not defeat market. Defense closing plan: concede entry/deployment/temporary local hold where record supports it; center exact market phrase 'commenced a military offensive intended to establish control.' Plaintiff proves controversial deployment and temporary control but not qualifying offensive intent. Use admitted record language: temporary, defensive, limited, few points, limited capacity east of Line A, after Assad collapse and Syrian army abandonment, prevent hostile force embedding, until suitable/security arrangement. Argue plaintiff's reading makes 'offensive' and 'intended' redundant and would convert any temporary defensive buffer-zone hold into a qualifying invasion. Provenance limitations matter only to weight, not as sole basis. No new evidence/reports allowed or needed in closing.
